parsed_review.summary: CP00-684 is a descriptor-only bridge pack (RP22 External Integrations I P06 Claude-review/closeout tail + P07 failure-recovery foundation; 150 units, risk C) and correctly claims production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false pending exactly one valid hardened read-only Claude review. The claim is well supported: (1) manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, and adjudication consistently report pending_review / not-production-ready with no contradictory enterprise-trust or runtime claim; (2) all 22 validation commands in command-evidence.json passed (exit 0), including all 10 required by the brief; (3) registry.js CP684 binding/requirements match the baseline exactly (deliverable, phase, micro-phase, and micro-title distributions all sum to 150 and match the prompt), and service.js builds a descriptor whose every row is descriptor_only with all runtime flags false, validated by validators.js and exercised by model.test.js plus the contract-projection chain; (4) the cp684 no-write attestation is comprehensive (no real data, no writes, no leaks, customer-safe errors only), Hermes is used only for deterministic packet/receipt handling (hermes_runtime_opened=false, runtime_receipt_emitted=false), and the authority boundary keeps Claude read-only and non-final while requiring human final approval for enterprise trust / runtime opening at CP00-685. Review-hardening preconditions are satisfied: auth preflight passed with non-zero valid-JSON stdout (1436 bytes), prompt within size limit, tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob, schema present, and summary-only review correctly disallowed for this Risk-C pack. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One trivial P3 documentation-scope observation is recorded (changed-file-scope.json lists docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md in untracked_implementation_files while it is also, correctly, preserved as an unrelated dirty file), which does not affect the pack's authoritative scope lists. Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists new-session-handoff.md as both an untracked implementation file and an unrelated preserved file
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appears in untracked_implementation_files (line 184) and also in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 191). This dual classification is cosmetically confusing. However, it is non-load-bearing: the file is excluded from the authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs and active_pack_required_untracked_files lists, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (corroborated by validation-report.json), and the manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved also classifies it as unrelated. The pack's required untracked files remain exactly the five docs/closeout-packs/cp00-684/ artifacts, so there is no pack-scope leak or omission.
findings.recommendation: Optional only: exclude ops/handoff doc files under docs/closeout-pack-plan/ from the untracked_implementation_files heuristic so a file is not simultaneously listed as an implementation file and an unrelated preserved file. No action is required for CP00-684 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational, non-blocking. Operative active-pack scope lists correctly exclude the file and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so pack scope is intact. Does not affect the production_ready=false claim or any boundary.
